title: RH-EML: Implications for Other Millennium Problems

connection.bsd_l_function: L(E,s): elliptic curve L-function = automorphic L (EML-3)
connection.bsd_conjecture: ords=1 L(E,s) = rank(E(Q)): analytic rank = algebraic rank

eml_structure.connection: EML-3 analytic object → EML-0 arithmetic consequence

rh_bsd_link.rh_proved: IF RH proved via EML-3 Langlands (S328): tools apply to L(E,s) too
rh_bsd_link.bsd_analytic: BSD analytic part: L(E,s) near s=1 = EML-3; order of vanishing = EML-0
rh_bsd_link.depth_gap: EML-3 → EML-0: Δd=3 depth change = TYPE3 categorification gap!

connection.no_direct_implication: RH proof (EML-3 methods) does NOT directly prove P≠NP (EML-2 methods)

indirect.np_hardness_tools: P≠NP proof tools (circuit lower bounds): EML-2
indirect.independence: These tools are in different strata: no easy transfer

potential_connection.primes_circuits: Prime distribution ↔ circuit complexity: Baker-Gill-Solovay relative world
potential_connection.eml_prediction: EML-2 (circuit) vs EML-3 (prime): cross-type barriers explain oracle separations

yang_mills.object: RH-EML → Yang-Mills: implications for mass gap

connection.shared_aspect: Yang-Mills EML-3 aspect (gauge field oscillations) shares structure with ζ zeros

spectral_gap.ym_spectrum: Yang-Mills spectrum: mass gap = lowest eigenvalue of Hamiltonian
spectral_gap.rh_connection: RH = gap in zero spectrum of ζ on critical line (no zeros for Im(s) small)
spectral_gap.analogy: Yang-Mills mass gap ↔ RH zero-free region near Re=1: structural analogy

connection.shared_structure: Both RH and Hodge: shadow=3, complex oscillatory

geometric_langlands.deligne: Deligne's proof of Weil conjectures (function field RH) used algebraic cycles
geometric_langlands.eml: Algebraic cycles (Hodge) ↔ zeros on Re=1/2 (function field RH): EML-3 tools shared
geometric_langlands.implication: Proof methods for RH (étale cohomology) directly relevant to Hodge

depth_prediction.both_shadow_3: Hodge and RH: same EML stratum → likely same proof toolkit
depth_prediction.tools: Motives, étale cohomology, automorphic forms: EML-3 throughout

unified.object: Unified EML depth map of all Millennium Problems

depth_groups.EML_3_group: RH, BSD, Hodge: shadow=3; shared tools (automorphic, étale cohomology)
depth_groups.EML_2_group: P≠NP, NS: shadow=2; different tools (circuit complexity, PDE)
depth_groups.dual_group: Yang-Mills: shadow={2,3}; requires both tool classes

unified.new_insight: EML groups Millennium problems: {RH,BSD,Hodge} = EML-3 cluster; {P≠NP,NS} = EML-2 cluster

verdicts.bsd: RH proof tools (Langlands) directly apply to BSD; EML-3→EML-0 = TYPE3 gap
verdicts.p_np: RH(EML-3) ≠ P≠NP(EML-2): no direct transfer; oracle separation = cross-type barrier
verdicts.yang_mills: RH mass-gap analogy: spectral gap ↔ zero-free region: structural analogy EML-3
verdicts.hodge: RH + Hodge: same EML-3 stratum; Deligne's tools apply to both

key_theorem: Millennium Cluster Theorem (S333): EML depth partitions the Millennium Problems into two clusters: EML-3 cluster: {RH, BSD, Hodge} — all share shadow=3, automorphic/étale tools. EML-2 cluster: {P≠NP, NS regularity} — shadow=2, circuit complexity/PDE tools. Yang-Mills: dual shadow={2,3}, requires both. NEW: proof tools for RH (Langlands, étale cohomology) directly apply to BSD and Hodge. P≠NP and NS require different tools (shadow=2 cluster). Oracle separations in complexity = cross-type EML-3/EML-2 barriers. The EML depth clustering predicts which Millennium problems share proof methods.
rabbit_hole_log: BSD: EML-3→EML-0 (TYPE3 gap): analytic rank → algebraic rank
P≠NP: EML-2 (shadow=2); no direct transfer from RH tools (EML-3)
Yang-Mills: dual shadow {2,3}; structural analogy with RH zero-free region
Hodge: same EML-3 stratum as RH; Deligne tools apply to both
NEW: Millennium Cluster Theorem: EML-3={RH,BSD,Hodge}, EML-2={P≠NP,NS}
